5. Report this run's exact hackathon log path and thread ID.
No commit.

[Data](./!INDEX.md)\\r\\n   4: \\r\\n   5: ## Purpose\\r\\n   6: \\r\\n   7: This document describes the current Space Rocks source-of-truth and data-sync pipeline.\\r\\n   8: \\r\\n   9: It covers the project-wide data ownership boundaries, the active `tools/data_sync` generation workflow, generated outputs, validation commands, and the adjacent source-of-truth paths that are intentionally not owned by data-sync.\\r\\n  10: \\r\\n  11: ## Overview\\r\\n  12: \\r\\n  13: Space Rocks uses editable shared source files to keep client, server, player-data, and API contracts aligned.\\r\\n  14: \\r\\n  15: The active `tools/data_sync` pipeline currently owns generation and drift checks for:\\r\\n  16: \\r\\n  17: ```text\\r\\n  18: constants\\r\\n  19: packets\\r\\n  20: drop_tables\\r\\n  21: realtime_wire\\r\\n  22: observability\\r\\n  23: ```\\r\\n  24: \\r\\n  25: The data-sync tool reads TOML sources under `shared/`, renders deterministic language outputs, and either writes them, diffs them, or verifies that generated outputs are current.\\r\\n  26: \\r\\n  27: The broader project also has source-of-truth files that sit beside data-sync but are not generated by it. Those include OpenAPI HTTP contracts, collision-shape exports, asteroid variant data, Rails migrations, Godot scenes, and player-data physical storage schemas.\\r\\n  28: \\r\\n  29: The important rule is:\\r\\n  30: \\r\\n  31: ```text\\r\\n  32: Edit the owning source-of-truth file.\\r\\n  33: Regenerate or validate the derived outputs.\\r\\n  34: Do not hand-edit generated files as authority.\\r\\n  35: Do not treat one source-of-truth domain as owning another.\\r\\n  36: ```\\r\\n  37: \\r\\n  38: ## Source files\\r\\n  39: \\r\\n  40: Active data-sync constants sources:\\r\\n  41: \\r\\n  42: ```text\\r\\n  43: shared/constants/server_constants.toml\\r\\n  44: shared/constants/server_entities.toml\\r\\n  45: shared/constants/weapons.toml\\r\\n  46: shared/constants/client/presentation.toml\\r\\n  47: shared/constants/client/shell.toml\\r\\n  48: shared/constants/client/lobby.toml\\r\\n  49: shared/constants/pickups.toml\\r\\n  50: shared/constants/weapon_pickups.toml\\r\\n  51: ```\\r\\n  52: \\r\\n  53: Active data-sync packet sources:\\r\\n  54: \\r\\n  55: ```text\\r\\n  56: shared/packets/outputs.toml\\r\\n  57: shared/packets/gameplay.toml\\r\\n  58: shared/packets/debug.toml\\r\\n  59: shared/packets/lobby.toml\\r\\n  60: shared/packets/webrtc.toml\\r\\n  61: shared/packets/player_data.toml\\r\\n  62: ```\\r\\n  63: \\r\\n  64: Physical realtime-wire source:\\r\\n  65: \\r\\n  66: ```text\\r\\n  67: shared/packets/realtime_wire.toml\\r\\n  68: ```\\r\\n  69: \\r\\n  70: Active data-sync drop-table sources:\\r\\n  71: \\r\\n  72: ```text\\r\\n  73: shared/drop_tables/basicasteroids.toml\\r\\n  74: ```\\r\\n  75: \\r\\n  76: Player-data logical schema sources validated by data-sync:\\r\\n  77: \\r\\n  78: ```text\\r\\n  79: shared/player_data/stats.toml\\r\\n  80: shared/player_data/match_result.toml\\r\\n  81: ```\\r\\n  82: \\r\\n  83: Observability contract sources:\\r\\n  84: \\r\\n  85: ```text\\r\\n  86: shared/contracts/observability/schema.toml\\r\\n  87: shared/contracts/observability/services.toml\\r\\n  88: shared/contracts/observability/events.toml\\r\\n  89: shared/contracts/observability/fields.toml\\r\\n  90: shared/contracts/observability/redaction.toml\\r\\n  91: shared/contracts/observability/retention_tiers.toml\\r\\n  92: shared/contracts/observability/diagnostic_bundle.toml\\r\\n  93: ```\\r\\n  94: \\r\\n  95: Adjacent source-of-truth files not owned by data-sync generation:\\r\\n  96: \\r\\n  97: ```text\\r\\n  98: shared/contracts/http/openapi.yaml\\r\\n  99: shared/collisions/collision_shapes.json\\r\\n 100: shared/asteroids/variants.toml\\r\\n 101: services/api-server/db/migrate/\\r\\n 102: client/scenes/\\r\\n 103: ```\\r\\n 104: \\r\\n 105: ## Configuration\\r\\n 106: \\r\\n 107: The default data-sync configuration is:\\r\\n 108: \\r\\n 109: ```text\\r\\n 110: tools/data_sync/config.toml\\r\\n 111: ```\\r\\n 112: \\r\\n 113: That configuration defines:\\r\\n 114: \\r\\n 115: ```text\\r\\n 116: [sot.constants]\\r\\n 117: [sot.packets]\\r\\n 118: [sot.drop_tables]\\r\\n 119: [sot.player_data]\\r\\n 120: [sot.realtime_wire]\\r\\n 121: [sot.observability]\\r\\n 122: [realtime_wire.go]\\r\\n 123: [realtime_wire.gds]\\r\\n 124: [realtime_wire.json]\\r\\n 125: [realtime_wire.docs]\\r\\n 126: [constants.scan]\\r\\n 127: [packets.go]\\r\\n 128: [packets.gds]\\r\\n 129: [packets.ts]\\r\\n 130: [drop_tables.go]\\r\\n 131: [observability.go]\\r\\n 132: [observability.gds]\\r\\n 133: [observability.ruby]\\r\\n 134: [observability.json]\\r\\n 135: [observability.docs]\\r\\n 136: ```\\r\\n 137: \\r\\n 138: `[sot.*]` sections define source-of-truth input paths.\\r\\n 139: \\r\\n 140: `[constants.scan]` defines where constants destination blocks are discovered. Current scan targets are:\\r\\n 141: \\r\\n 142: ```text\\r\\n 143: services/game-server/internal/constants/*.go\\r\\n 144: client/scripts/generated/constants/*.gd\\r\\n 145: ```\\r\\n 146: \\r\\n 147: Packet target configuration defines generated packet output groups. The packet output IDs are resolved against `shared/packets/outputs.toml`.\\r\\n 148: \\r\\n 149: Current packet output IDs are:\\r\\n 150: \\r\\n 151: ```text\\r\\n 152: server_entities_packets\\r\\n 153: server_game_packets\\r\\n 154: server_devtools_packets\\r\\n 155: player_data_packets\\r\\n 156: client_packets\\r\\n 157: ```\\r\\n 158: \\r\\n 159: Realtime-wire targets are direct configured outputs rather than packet output IDs.\\r\\n 160: \\r\\n 161: TypeScript packet output is configured but disabled:\\r\\n 162: \\r\\n 163: ```text\\r\\n 164: [packets.ts]\\r\\n 165: enabled = false\\r\\n 166: ```\\r\\n 167: \\r\\n 168: Drop tables currently generate Go output only.\\r\\n 169: \\r\\n 170: ## Generated outputs\\r\\n 171: \\r\\n 172: Constants outputs:\\r\\n 173: \\r\\n 174: ```text\\r\\n 175: client/scripts/generated/constants/constants.gd\\r\\n 176: services/game-server/internal/constants/constants.go\\r\\n 177: services/game-server/internal/constants/weapons.go\\r\\n 178: services/game-server/internal/constants/powerups.go\\r\\n 179: services/game-server/internal/constants/weapon_pickups.go\\r\\n 180: ```\\r\\n 181: \\r\\n 182: Packet outputs:\\r\\n 183: \\r\\n 184: ```text\\r\\n 185: client/scripts/generated/networking/packets/packets.gd\\r\\n 186: services/game-server/internal/game/runtime/packets_generated.go\\r\\n 187: services/game-server/internal/game/packets.go\\r\\n 188: services/game-server/internal/devtools/packets_generated.go\\r\\n 189: services/player-data/protocol/packets.go\\r\\n 190: ```\\r\\n 191: \\r\\n 192: Realtime-wire outputs:\\r\\n 193: \\r\\n 194: ```text\\r\\n 195: services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtimewire/generated.go\\r\\n 196: client/scripts/generated/networking/realtime_wire_generated.gd\\r\\n 197: shared/packets/generated/realtime_wire.json\\r\\n 198: docs/protocol/generated/realtime-wire-reference.md\\r\\n 199: ```\\r\\n 200: \\r\\n 201: Drop-table outputs:\\r\\n 202: \\r\\n 203: ```text\\r\\n 204: services/game-server/internal/game/drops/drop_tables.go\\r\\n 205: ```\\r\\n 206: \\r\\n 207: Observability outputs:\\r\\n 208: \\r\\n 209: ```text\\r\\n 210: shared/go/observabilityevent/contract_generated.go\\r\\n 211: client/scripts/generated/observability/contract_generated.gd\\r\\n 212: services/api-server/app/lib/observability/contract_generated.rb\\r\\n 213: shared/contracts/observability/generated/contract.json\\r\\n 214: docs/observability/generated/contract-reference.md\\r\\n 215: ```\\r\\n 216: \\r\\n 217: Adjacent generated or exported outputs outside data-sync:\\r\\n 218: \\r\\n 219: ```text\\r\\n 220: shared/collisions/collision_shapes.json\\r\\n 221: client/scripts/generated/asteroids/asteroid_variants.gd\\r\\n 222: services/game-server/internal/game/asteroids/variants.go\\r\\n 223: services/game-server/internal/game/physics/collision_shapes.go\\r\\n 224: ```\\r\\n 225: \\r\\n 226: Generated files are not independent sources. If generated output is stale, update the owning source and rerun the relevant pipeline command.\\r\\n 227: \\r\\n 228: ## Consumers\\r\\n 229: \\r\\n 230: The Godot client consumes generated constants and packet helpers through:\\r\\n 231: \\r\\n 232: ```text\\r\\n 233: client/scripts/generated/constants/constants.gd\\r\\n 234: client/scripts/generated/networking/packets/packets.gd\\r\\n 235: ```\\r\\n 236: \\r\\n 237: The game server consumes generated constants, packet structs, packet constants, devtools packet structs, realtime lane state-shape and packet-shape outputs, and drop-table code through:\\r\\n 238: \\r\\n 239: ```text\\r\\n 240: services/game-server/internal/constants/\\r\\n 241: services/game-server/internal/game/packets.go\\r\\n 242: services/game-server/internal/game/runtime/packets_generated.go\\r\\n 243: services/game-server/internal/devtools/packets_generated.go\\r\\n 244: services/game-server/internal/game/drops/drop_tables.go\\r\\n 245: ```\\r\\n 246: \\r\\n 247: Realtime-wire descriptor consumers are:\\r\\n 248: \\r\\n 249: ```text\\r\\n 250: services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/compact_wire_descriptor.go  server descriptor encoder\\r\\n 251: client/scripts/protocol/realtime/compact_wire_descriptor_decoder.gd          client descriptor decoder\\r\\n 252: services/game-server/internal/protocol/realtime/quantize/                    server quantization lookup\\r\\n 253: client/scripts/protocol/realtime/realtime_quantize.gd                         client quantization lookup\\r\\n 254: ```\\r\\n 255: \\r\\n 256: The player-data service consumes generated packet contracts through:\\r\\n 257: \\r\\n 258: ```text\\r\\n 259: services/player-data/protocol/packets.go\\r\\n 260: ```\\r\\n 261: \\r\\n 262: The Rails API server consumes HTTP contract source from:\\r\\n 263: \\r\\n 264: ```text\\r\\n 265: shared/contracts/http/openapi.yaml\\r\\n 266: ```\\r\\n 267: \\r\\n 268: The Rails API server does not consume data-sync generated output for HTTP controllers or migrations.\\r\\n 269: \\r\\n 270: ## Pipeline usage\\r\\n 271: \\r\\n 272: Exactly one data-sync operation must be selected:\\r\\n 273: \\r\\n 274: ```text\\r\\n 275: -push\\r\\n 276: -pull\\r\\n 277: -diff\\r\\n 278: -check\\r\\n 279: -validate\\r\\n 280: ```\\r\\n 281: \\r\\n 282: Supported active generation domains are:\\r\\n 283: \\r\\n 284: ```text\\r\\n 285: -constants\\r\\n 286: -packets\\r\\n 287: -drop-tables\\r\\n 288: -realtime-wire\\r\\n 289: -observability\\r\\n 290: ```\\r\\n 291: \\r\\n 292: `-player_data` is currently validation-only.\\r\\n 293: \\r\\n 294: Supported language flags are:\\r\\n 295: \\r\\n 296: ```text\\r\\n 297: -go\\r\\n 298: -gds\\r\\n 299: -ts\\r\\n 300: -ruby\\r\\n 301: ```\\r\\n 302: \\r\\n 303: For `-realtime-wire`, `-go`, `-gds`, `-json`, and `-docs` select the generated outputs. For `-observability`, `-go`, `-gds`, `-ruby`, `-json`, and `-docs` select the generated outputs. `-json` and `-docs` apply to both realtime wire and observability; they are output selectors, not programming languages or general language targets for every domain.\\r\\n 304: \\r\\n 305: `-push` writes generated output.\\r\\n 306: \\r\\n 307: ```text\\r\\n 308: data-sync -push -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 309: data-sync -push -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 310: data-sync -push -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 311: data-sync -push -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 312: data-sync -push -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 313: ```\\r\\n 314: \\r\\n 315: `-diff` renders generated output and prints a unified diff without writing.\\r\\n 316: \\r\\n 317: ```text\\r\\n 318: data-sync -diff -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 319: data-sync -diff -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 320: data-sync -diff -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 321: data-sync -diff -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 322: data-sync -diff -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 323: ```\\r\\n 324: \\r\\n 325: `-check` renders generated output, writes nothing, exits `0` when outputs are current, and exits `1` when generated files differ.\\r\\n 326: \\r\\n 327: ```text\\r\\n 328: data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 329: data-sync -check -packets -go -gds\\r\\n 330: data-sync -check -drop-tables -go\\r\\n 331: data-sync -check -realtime-wire -go -gds -json -docs\\r\\n 332: data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n 333: ```\\r\\n 334: \\r\\n 335: `-validate` checks configuration, source TOML structure, supported values, configured files, managed blocks, packet schemas, drop-table schemas, and player-data logical schema where requested.\\r\\n 336: \\r\\n 337: ```text\\r\\n 338: data-sync -validate\\r\\n 339: data-sync -validate -constants\\r\\n 340: data-sync -validate -packets\\r\\n 341: data-sync -validate -realtime-wire\\r\\n 342: data-sync -validate -drop-tables\\r\\n 343: data-sync -validate -player_data\\r\\n 344: data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n 345: ```\\r\\n 346: \\r\\n 347: `-pull` is restricted to constants and one language at a time.\\r\\n 348: \\r\\n 349: ```text\\r\\n 350: data-sync -pull -constants -go\\r\\n 351: data-sync -pull -constants -gds\\r\\n 352: ```\\r\\n 353: \\r\\n 354: Packet pull is intentionally unsupported. Packet shape changes must be made in `shared/packets/*.toml` and pushed outward.\\r\\n 355: \\r\\n 356: Drop-table pull is not the active workflow. Drop-table changes should be made in `shared/drop_tables/*.toml`.\\r\\n 357: \\r\\n 358: Player-data schema push/pull/diff/check is not implemented. `-player_data` is validation-only.\\r\\n 359: \\r\\n 360: ## Validation commands\\r\\n 361: \\r\\n 362: Project-wide data-sync validation:\\r\\n 363: \\r\\n 364: ```text\\r\\n 365: data-sync -validate\\r\\n 366: ```\\r\\n 367: \\r\\n 368: Constants drift check:\\r\\n 369: \\r\\n 370: ```text\\r\\n 371: data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n 372: ```\\r\\n 373: \\r\\n 374: Packet drift check:\\r\\n 375: \\r\\n 376: ```text\\r\\n data-sync -validate -player_data\\r\\n 398: data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n 399: ```\\r\\n 400: \\r\\n 401: Tool test suite:\\r\\n 402: \\r\\n 403: ```text\\r\\n 404: cd tools/data_sync && python -m pytest\\r\\n 405: ```\\r\\n 406: \\r\\n 407: Game-server validation after generated server constants, packets, drop tables, asteroid data, or collision data change:\\r\\n 408: \\r\\n 409: ```text\\r\\n 410: cd services/game-server && go test -buildvcs=false ./...\\r\\n 411: ```\\r\\n 412: \\r\\n 413: Player-data validation after player-data packets or logical player-data schema ```text\\r\\n 422: cd services/api-server && bundle exec rails test test/contracts/openapi_contract_test.rb\\r\\n 423: ```\\r\\n 424: \\r\\n 425: Collision-shape export validation uses the Godot export tool, not data-sync:\\r\\n 426: \\r\\n 427: ```text\\r\\n 428: godot --headless --path client -s res://tools/export_collision_shapes.gd\\r\\n 429: ```\\r\\n 430: \\r\\n 431: ## Failure modes\\r\\n 432: \\r\\n 433: Duplicate constants sections across multiple source TOML files fail validation or generation. Each constants section must have exactly one source owner.\\r\\n 434: \\r\\n 435: Missing constants sections fail when a generated destination block references a section that does not exist in the constants source set.\\r\\n 436: \\r\\n 437: Malformed or ambiguous managed blocks fail constants sync. Missing start markers, missing end markers, duplicate blocks, or mismatched marker comment styles are hard failures.\\r\\n 438: \\r\\n 439: Unsupported constants values fail validation. Constants support booleans, integers, floats, strings, recursively formatted GDScript arrays and string-keyed dictionaries, including structured ICE-server-shaped values with nested URL arrays. A two-number numeric array remains a `Vector2` value for GDScript output. Unsupported nested values and non-string dictionary keys fail GDScript generation.\\r\\n 440: \\r\\n 441: Packet schema errors fail validation or generation. Common failures include duplicate packet type IDs, duplicate packet values, invalid struct names, invalid JSON field names, unsupported field types, unknown struct references, unknown packet output IDs, or references to unknown packet builder fields.\\r\\n 442: \\r\\n 443: Packet target errors fail generation. A configured packet target must be enabled, must have a matching output in `shared/packets/outputs.toml`, and must use a supported language renderer.\\r\\n 444: \\r\\n 445: Realtime-wire validation fails for invalid logical packet, struct, or field references; alias collisions; invalid tuple layouts; missing quantization, ID, or event declarations; and inconsistent packet, lane, record, or compatibility metadata. Generated drift is reported by the realtime-wire check command.\\r\\n 446: \\r\\n 447: Physical realtime-wire declarations that reference unknown logical packet, struct, or field names fail validation.\\r\\n 448: \\r\\n 449: Duplicate or non-invertible aliases fail validation.\\r\\n 450: \\r\\n 451: Invalid record, fixed-tuple, sparse-tuple, or sparse-positional layouts fail validation.\\r\\n 452: \\r\\n 453: Missing or invalid quantization, ID codec or selector, event, or decode-alternative references fail validation.\\r\\n 454: \\r\\n 455: Generated Go, GDScript, JSON, or documentation drift is reported by the realtime-wire check command.\\r\\n 456: \\r\\n 457: Observability validation fails for malformed contract TOML, duplicate services/events/fields, invalid service allowlists or trace requirements, unsupported field types or limits, ambiguous redaction rules, unknown retention/diagnostic-bundle references, missing configured targets, or non-deterministic generated output. Its generated Go, GDScript, Ruby, JSON, and Markdown drift is reported by the observability check command. Runtime rejection/write failures are bounded emitter outcomes and are distinct from data-sync failures.\\r\\n 458: \\r\\n 459: Packet pull fails by design. Packet schemas are edited in TOML, not reconstructed from generated Go or GDScript.\\r\\n 460: \\r\\n 461: Drop-table generation is Go-only. `-drop-tables` requires `-go`.\\r\\n 462: \\r\\n 463: Player-data schema currently validates only a limited logical schema shape. Supported player-data field types are:\\r\\n 464: \\r\\n 465: ```text\\r\\n 466: string\\r\\n 467: integer\\r\\n 468: boolean\\r\\n 469: ```\\r\\n 470: \\r\\n 471: Each player-data field must define `required`, `optional`, or `default`.\\r\\n 472: \\r\\n 473: TypeScript packet output is disabled in the default configuration. A command that tries to generate disabled packet targets should fail instead of silently producing partial output.\\r\\n 474: \\r\\n 475: Editing generated files directly creates drift. The correct fix is to update the owning source and rerun the appropriate data-sync command.\\r\\n 476: \\r\\n 477: Treating OpenAPI, Rails migrations, collision exports, or Godot scene data as data-sync-owned material creates ownership confusion. Those are adjacent source-of-truth paths with separate validation workflows.\\r\\n 478: \\r\\n 479: ## Code or source map\\r\\n 480: \\r\\n 481: CLI entry and orchestration:\\r\\n 482: \\r\\n 483: It does not imply every source of truth is handled by `tools/data_sync`.\\r\\n 614: \\r\\n 615: The data-sync pipeline is intentionally narrower than the project-wide source-of-truth map. Constants, packets, drop tables, realtime wire, and observability are active generated domains. Player-data logical schema is validate-only. HTTP contracts, collision exports, asteroid variants, Rails migrations, and Godot scenes use separate ownership and verification paths.\\r\\n===== docs/data/observability-contract.md =====\\r\\n   1: # Observability Contract\\r\\n   2: \\r\\n   3: Parent index: [Data](./!INDEX.md)\\r\\n   4: \\r\\n   5: ## Purpose and ownership\\r\\n   6: \\r\\n   7: Observability is an active `tools/data_sync` source-of-truth domain. The editable contract is the TOML source set below. `data-sync` validates the cross-file model and generates all language/runtime metadata and the generated contract reference. Runtime emitters consume generated metadata; generated outputs are never edited manually.\\r\\n   8: \\r\\n   9: ## Source files\\r\\n  10: \\r\\n  11: ```text\\r\\n  12: shared/contracts/observability/schema.toml\\r\\n  13: shared/contracts/observability/services.toml\\r\\n  14: shared/contracts/observability/events.toml\\r\\n  15: shared/contracts/observability/fields.toml\\r\\n  16: shared/contracts/observability/redaction.toml\\r\\n  17: shared/contracts/observability/retention_tiers.toml\\r\\n  18: shared/contracts/observability/diagnostic_bundle.toml\\r\\n  19: ```\\r\\n  20: \\r\\n  21: Together these files define the envelope, service registry, event eligibility and defaults, context/free-form fields, redaction policy, retention tiers, and diagnostic-bundle contract. No service may add a local event definition or policy that changes these generated rules.\\r\\n  22: \\r\\n  23: ## Generated outputs\\r\\n  24: \\r\\n  25: ```text\\r\\n  26: shared/go/observabilityevent/contract_generated.go\\r\\n  27: client/scripts/generated/observability/contract_generated.gd\\r\\n  28: services/api-server/app/lib/observability/contract_generated.rb\\r\\n  29: shared/contracts/observability/generated/contract.json\\r\\n  30: docs/observability/generated/contract-reference.md\\r\\n  31: ```\\r\\n  32: \\r\\n  33: The generated Markdown reference is a read-only rendered reference. It changes only through the observability data-sync generator.\\r\\n  34: \\r\\n  35: ## Commands\\r\\n  36: \\r\\n  37: From the repository root:\\r\\n  38: \\r\\n  39: ```bash\\r\\n  40: data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n  41: data-sync -diff -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n  42: data-sync -push -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n  43: data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n  44: ```\\r\\n  45: \\r\\n  46: `-validate` reads and cross-validates the source contract without writing. `-diff` renders every selected output and reports drift. `-push` writes selected generated outputs. `-check` renders without writing and fails when any selected output differs.\\r\\n  47: \\r\\n  48: ## Validation rules\\r\\n  49: \\r\\n  50: Validation covers:\\r\\n  51: \\r\\n  52: - one valid schema/envelope definition and supported schema version;\\r\\n  53: - unique service keys and emitted names;\\r\\n  54: - unique event names, categories, levels, service allowlists, trace requirements, and bridge-only declarations;\\r\\n  55: - context field names, types, UUID requirements, and limits;\\r\\n  56: - free-form key/value/count/size limits;\\r\\n  57: - redaction exact/fragment rules and rejection of ambiguous actions;\\r\\n  58: - retention tier references and file policy values;\\r\\n  59: - diagnostic-bundle field/event references;\\r\\n  60: - deterministic output ordering and complete configured target files.\\r\\n  61: \\r\\n  62: Generation fails rather than silently dropping an unsupported language or output. The Ruby selector is observability-specific; JSON and docs selectors apply to observability as well as realtime wire.\\r\\n  63: \\r\\n  64: ## Generation ownership and consumers\\r\\n  65: \\r\\n  66: `tools/data_sync/config.toml` owns the `[sot.observability]` source list and the `observability.go`, `observability.gds`, `observability.ruby`, `observability.json`, and `observability.docs` targets. The tool implementation and generators are the only owners of generated output content.\\r\\n  67: \\r\\n  68: Consumers are:\\r\\n  69: \\r\\n  70: - Go services through `shared/go/observabilityevent` and service `logging.Emit` boundaries;\\r\\n  71: - the Godot client through `observability_emitter.gd` and generated GDScript constants;\\r\\n  72: - API Rails through `Observability::Emitter`, `WorkerRuntime`, and `PumaHooks`;\\r\\n  73: - diagnostic-aggregator through generated Go observability metadata;\\r\\n  74: - handwritten service/data docs through this document and the generated contract reference.\\r\\n  75: \\r\\n  76: ## Failure modes\\r\\n  77: \\r\\n  78: - malformed TOML or a missing source file fails validation;\\r\\n  79: - duplicate service/event/field/retention identifiers fail validation;\\r\\n  80: - an event referencing an unknown service, field, or retention tier fails validation;\\r\\n  81: - invalid trace/UUID, field type, redaction, or limit declarations fail validation;\\r\\n  82: - unsupported selector combinations fail CLI parsing;\\r\\n  83: - generated output drift causes `-check` to exit nonzero;\\r\\n  84: - a runtime emitter rejects an event, unsafe value, or oversized record according to generated rejection codes;\\r\\n  85: - a writer failure degrades the service-owned runtime but must not cause the workflow to raise or serialize the rejected/private payload.\\r\\n  86: \\r\\n  87: The contract pipeline and runtime writer failures are separate: data-sync failure blocks a generated-contract change; a runtime write failure is a bounded service degradation.\\r\\n  88: \\r\\n  89: ## Rule against manual edits\\r\\n  90: \\r\\n  91: Do not edit any generated output directly. Change the owning TOML source, run validation, review the diff, push the selected outputs, and run the check command. A manual edit to a generated Go, GDScript, Ruby, JSON, or Markdown file is drift and must be overwritten by the next push.\\r\\n  92: \\r\\n  93: ## Code and test map\\r\\n  94: \\r\\n  95: ```text\\r\\n  96: tools/data_sync/config.toml\\r\\n  97: tools/data_sync/main.py\\r\\n  98: tools/data_sync/data_sync/cli.py\\r\\n  99: tools/data_sync/data_sync/config.py\\r\\n 100: tools/data_sync/data_sync/observability_toml.py\\r\\n 101: tools/data_sync/data_sync/observability_validate.py\\r\\n 102: tools/data_sync/data_sync/observability_sync.py\\r\\n 103: tools/data_sync/data_sync/model/observability.py\\r\\n 104: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_go.py\\r\\n 105: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_gds.py\\r\\n 106: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_ruby.py\\r\\n 107: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_json.py\\r\\n 108: tools/data_sync/data_sync/generators/observability_docs.py\\r\\n 109: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_toml.py\\r\\n 110: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_validate.py\\r\\n 111: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_sync.py\\r\\n 112: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_generators.py\\r\\n 113: tools/data_sync/tests/test_observability_emission_guards.py\\r\\n 114: ```\\r\\n 115: \\r\\n 116: ## Related docs\\r\\n 117: \\r\\n 118: - [Data sync and SSoT pipeline](data-sync-and-ssot-pipeline.md)\\r\\n 119: - [Source-of-truth map](source-of-truth-map.md)\\r\\n 120: - [Canonical event emission](../observability/canonical-event-emission.md)\\r\\n 121: - [Generated contract reference](../observability/generated/contract-reference.md)\\r\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_5\",\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"The benchmark contains exactly 37 entries: 20 for the data-sync/SSoT document and 17 for the observability contract. Developer Onboarding\\r\\n5:## Purpose\\r\\n11:This document is not the source of truth for detailed system behavior. Detailed facts belong in the owning service, protocol, data, devtools, domain, systems-design, planning, or limits docs.\\r\\n13:## Overview\\r\\n21:shared/                 Shared source-of-truth data\\r\\n22:tools/data_sync/        Constants, packet, and generated-output sync tooling\\r\\n32:## Basic Setup\\r\\n55:Git LFS           binary/source asset files\\r\\n76:## Verification quickstart\\r\\n102:## Repository Shape\\r\\n104:`client/` contains the active Godot client project: scenes, scripts, assets, tests, generated client packet helpers, and generated constants.\\r\\n110:`shared/` contains shared source files used by generators and multiple runtimes, including constants, packet schemas, collision data, and related source-of-truth material.\\r\\n112:`tools/data_sync/` contains the data-sync tooling used to validate, diff, and regenerate shared outputs.\\r\\n120:`SourceAssets/` contains local source art material and is ignored by Git.\\r\\n122:## Development Tools\\r\\n124:### data-sync\\r\\n126:`data-sync` is the repo generation and synchronization tool for shared data.\\r\\n128:It is used for packet schemas, constants, generated Go outputs, generated GDScript outputs, and related shared-source validation.\\r\\n136:Use data-sync when changing shared packet or constants sources. Do not hand-edit generated outputs as the source of truth.\\r\\n138:Observability is also an active data-sync domain. Edit only `shared/contracts/observability/*.toml`, then run:\\r\\n141:data-sync -validate -observability\\r\\n142:data-sync -diff -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n143:data-sync -push -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n144:data-sync -check -observability -go -gds -ruby -json -docs\\r\\n147:See [Observability Contract](data/observability-contract.md) for the source/output owner map.\\r\\n152:data-sync -check -packets -go -gds\\r\\n153:data-sync -check -constants -go -gds\\r\\n159:data-sync -diff -packets -go -gds\\r\\n160:data-sync -diff -constants -go -gds\\r\\n166:data-sync -push -packets -go -gds\\r\\n167:data-sync -push -constants -go -gds\\r\\n170:Use `data-sync` for generated outputs. Do not use it as a replacement for understanding ownership. The owning data docs explain the source files and generated outputs.\\r\\n174:`doc-ledger` maintains the generated sections in documentation indexes.\\r\\n191:Do not manually fight generated index blocks. Manual index text belongs outside the doc-ledger marker blocks. If an index is wrong, fix the underlying file/folder placement, doc-ledger configuration, or generated description source rather than hand-editing only the generated block.\\r\\n212:Use `bruno-api/` for real local API-server routes and `bruno-diagnostic-aggregator/` for hosted diagnostic aggregator checks. This benchmark measures the quality of ordinary missing-link suggestions from the current Demon Docs pipeline. It complements, and does not modify, the positive-only recall benchmark in [`research/codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json`](../codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json).

## Corpus and sampling

- Corpus: Space Rocks revision `3387c94d10fdb94008f27b404098f3e0c32d911c` (10 reviewed documents, 51 trusted links).
- Source run: all 51 trusted links were hidden with seed `space-rocks-precision-v1`; the run produced 267 unique suggestions, including 24 recovered trusted links and 243 ordinary unmatched suggestions.
- Sample: 150 unique document-target pairs in [`space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json`](space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json).
- Sampling: every document’s top five was retained where available; the remainder was selected deterministically with SHA-256 least-represented fill across document area, score bucket, primary evidence kind, and rank bucket.
- Labels were assigned after inspecting the referenced document section and target file or directory. Each row records document and target references, excerpts, target kind, a target fingerprint, evidence, and a concise rationale.

The three labels are mutually exclusive: `valid_missing_link` is correct for strict precision, `plausible_but_unnecessary` is accepted only for acceptance precision, and `incorrect` is junk.

## Reproduction

Generate the source suggestion report without changing Space Rocks:

```text
go run ./cmd/ddocs codemap benchmark --repo D:\!bin\space-rocks --trusted-links research\codemap-review\space-rocks-trusted-links.json --holdout-count 51 --seed space-rocks-precision-v1 --format json --output tmp\codemap-precision\full-hidden.json
```

Evaluate the labeled sample:

```text
go run ./cmd/ddocs codemap precision --benchmark research\codemap-precision\space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json --suggestions tmp\codemap-precision\full-hidden.json
```

The evaluator verifies that every sampled pair, score/evidence payload, and rank still exists in the source report before calculating metrics.

## Results

Evaluation date: 2026-07-19.

| --- | ---: |
| sample size | 150 |

| precision@3 | 73.33% |

### Breakdowns

| 21+ | 33 | 19 | 22 | 57.58% | 66.67% |

### Sampling coverage

The sample covers all 10 documents, all four score buckets, all four rank buckets, all three observed primary evidence kinds, and four document areas: data (26), devtools (41), protocol (33), and services (50). Subsystem counts are recorded in the evaluator’s JSON output and the row-level artifact.

## Limitations

This is a curated sample, not an unbiased estimate over every repository path. It is drawn from a full-hidden run of the 51-link trusted corpus, so it evaluates the current evidence pipeline under that benchmark setup and does not measure recall or unseen-document behavior. Directory suggestions are retained as ordinary candidates but are judged conservatively. Labels are auditable and source-pinned, but this artifact has one review pass rather than independent inter-rater agreement. The existing recall benchmark remains the appropriate measure for recovery of known links.

import json,collections,hashlib,os
p=r'research/codemap-precision/space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json'
d=json.load(open(p,encoding='utf-8'))['suggestions']
print('documents',collections.Counter(x['document'] for x in d))
# deterministic coverage selection: first min hash per doc; then greedily maximize uncovered evidence/score pairs
for x in d:
 x['_h']=hashlib.sha256((x['document']+'\\0'+x['target']).encode()).hexdigest()
selected=[]
for doc in sorted(set(x['document'] for x in d)):
 selected.append(min((x for x in d if x['document']==doc),key=lambda x:x['_h']))
seen={(x['score_bucket'],x['primary_evidence_kind']) for x in selected}
remaining=[x for x in d if x not in selected]
while len(selected)<20:
 def key(x):
  pair=(x['score_bucket'],x['primary_evidence_kind'])
  return (0 if pair not in seen else 1, x['_h'])
 x=min(remaining,key=key); selected.append(x); remaining.remove(x); seen.add((x['score_bucket'],x['primary_evidence_kind']))
print('selected')
for i,x in enumerate(selected,1): print(i,x['document'],x['target'],x['label'],x['score_bucket'],x['primary_evidence_kind'],x['_h'][:12])
print('coverage docs',len(set(x['document'] for x in selected)),'pairs',len(set((x['score_bucket'],x['primary_evidence_kind']) for x in selected)))
print('coverage score',collections.Counter(x['score_bucket'] for x in selected))
print('coverage ev',collections.Counter(x['primary_evidence_kind'] for x in selected))
